summary_one_line: A syntactically complete decimal character reference '&#<digits>;' whose terminating ';' lets the RAW!=';' loop take its false (exit) side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A decimal character reference of the form '&#' followed by one-or-more ASCII decimal digits ('0'-'9') and then a ';' terminator, so the scanning loop at parser.c:2352 reads digits each iteration (L2359 true) and finally sees RAW == ';' to exit the loop (the globally-blocked false side).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile / value_profile_cmplog winners resolve the branch in 8/10 trials and actually hit the false (exit) side: at the blocker line the winner shows F=53 hits where the loser shows F=0, and the winner reaches L2372 'if (RAW == ';')' with F=35 while the loser has F=77 only on the true side; the CMP_MAP gradient over the single-byte 'RAW != ;' compare and the digit-range compares at L2359 steadily rewards inputs whose bytes approach ';' (0x3b) and stay in the decimal-digit range.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive and cmplog blocked the branch in 10/10 and 8/10 trials respectively; at the blocker the loser only ever takes the true side (W F=53 vs L F=0) because its char-ref bytes never form a complete '&#<digits>;' that terminates the loop — e.g. loser seed 5 contains 'h&#I' where the byte after '&#' is 'I' (non-digit), driving the invalid-charref error break instead of digit-scan-then-';'.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/operand-distance bucket for the single-byte comparison RAW != ';' (and the digit-range compares at L2359). Inputs that bring the scanned byte closer to ';' (0x3b) or into '0'-'9' produce new CMP_MAP buckets and are preserved as corpus, giving a gradient toward a well-formed '&#<digits>;' that naive (edge-only) and cmplog (I2S substitution, but ';' is a constant not derived from a logged operand pair the input lacks) do not get. The delta in BOTH decisive pairs is value_profile, isolating CMP_MAP gradient as the enabling technique.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the loop-exit comparison 'while (RAW != ';')' in the decimal char-ref arm of xmlParseCharRef, and the false (exit) side is globally blocked.
The globally blocked side is the false branch (loop exit on finding ';').
Both decisive pairs have technique delta value_profile, so the enabling technique for the winner is value_profile in both.
Pair 2 also has delta value_profile (value_profile_cmplog over cmplog).
value_profile resolves 8/10 while naive blocks 10/10 at this branch.
At the blocker line the winner takes the false (exit) side 53 times while the loser takes it 0 times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2352  T=355 F=53  T=89 F=0  while (RAW != ';') { /* loop blocked by count */  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Only the winner reaches the ';' check at L2372 on the false side; the loser only hits the true side.
The decimal-digit range test at L2359 inside the loop is exercised, the per-iteration accumulation of decimal digits the loop scans.
A loser-blocking seed contains '&#' followed by a non-digit 'I', which cannot terminate the digit-scan loop with ';'.
value_profile's CMP_MAP encodes operand distance per CMP, and an input producing a closer-matching CMP pair adds a new bucket and is preserved as corpus.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP distance is a coarse Hamming-distance / matching-prefix encoding, which gives a gradient over the single-byte RAW vs ';' comparison.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The distance bucket is a coarse encoding of how close the two
operands were (Hamming distance bucket for `trace_cmp*`; matching-prefix
length for string/memory CMPs).

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile feedback), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory I2S-floor lineage check does not apply. Verification rests on per-role coverage and the technique delta: both decisive pairs carry delta value_profile (value_profile>naive and value_profile_cmplog>cmplog), and the winner is the only role taking the false/exit side at L2352 (F=53 vs F=0) and reaching L2372 (RAW == ';'). CMP_MAP feedback preserves inputs whose RAW byte is Hamming-closer to ';' and inside the decimal-digit range, which is the gradient that builds a complete '&#<digits>;'.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the decimal-char-ref loop ('&#' digits ';') in which value_profile fails to resolve the loop-exit side at a significantly higher rate than naive across trials, OR per-role coverage showing naive/cmplog also reach the false side of L2352 as often as value_profile.

weakest_evidence_point: The seeds stored at this branch are from value_profile/value_profile_cmplog (provenance) but byte diff offsets (0x00-0x0a) do not pinpoint the exact '&#<digits>;' substring, so the digit-then-';' construction is inferred from source plus the loser seed's malformed 'h&#I' rather than a clean winner '	'-style substring.
